tools.name: COSMICENERGY
tools.evidence_snippet: Mandiant identified novel operational technology (OT) / industrial control system (ICS)-oriented malware, which we track as COSMICENERGY,

tools.name: PIEHOP
tools.evidence_snippet: PIEHOP is a disruption tool written in Python and packaged with PyInstaller that is capable of connecting to a user-supplied remote MSSQL server for uploading files and issuing remote commands to a RTU.

tools.name: LIGHTWORK
tools.evidence_snippet: LIGHTWORK is a disruption tool written in C++ that implements the IEC-104 protocol to modify the state of RTUs over TCP.

ttps.evidence_snippet: PIEHOP expects its main function to be called via another Python file, supplying either the argument control=True  or upload=True .
PIEHOP’s entry point c018c54eff8fd0b9be50b5d419d80f21 (r3_iec104_control.py) calls PIEHOP’s main function, supplying the argument control=True .
LIGHTWORK takes the following positional command line arguments: <ip_address> <port> <command> [either ON (1) or OFF (0)].
An adversary could potentially instruct a control systems device to perform an action that will cause an Impact.

summary: Mandiant identified novel operational technology (OT) / industrial control system (ICS)-oriented malware, which we track as COSMICENERGY. The malware is designed to interact with IEC-104 devices and cause electric power disruption.
